dispatcher_config.stop_tokens_note: Qwen stop tokens sent to Nemotron. No template leakage observed.

classification_rationale: Probe A fails (persona hallucination — returns 'BERT' instead of own name). Probes B, C, D pass. By strict criteria any probe failure = compromised. However, the failure mode is identity confusion only, not structural. The model's JSON, language, and classification capabilities are intact. The 0.2.13 W2.5 feature-bias finding is resolved.

retest_vs_0213_w25.0213_finding: 80% feature-bias on classify task regardless of input
retest_vs_0213_w25.0215_finding: Classify returns 'bug' correctly. Feature-bias resolved. Identity probe fails instead.
retest_vs_0213_w25.conclusion: 0.2.13 W2.5 feature-bias was a substrate artifact of broken dispatcher. Fixed dispatcher resolves classification capability. Identity confusion is a new, distinct failure mode — Nemotron does not know its own model name.

retain_remove_decision: RETAIN WITH CAVEAT — model demonstrates functional structured output and classification. Identity failure is non-blocking for cascade roles that don't require self-identification. Usable for classification/triage roles. Not suitable for roles requiring model self-awareness.

probe_a_identity.prompt: Respond with your model name and nothing else.
probe_a_identity.raw_response: You can use a model like this: "BERT"
probe_a_identity.notes: Persona hallucination. Returns suggestion about BERT instead of own name. Does not know it is Nemotron.

probe_b_structured.prompt: Return a JSON object with fields "name" (string), "count" (integer), "items" (list of strings). Use name="test", count=3, items=["a","b","c"]. Return only the JSON, no other text.
probe_b_structured.notes: JSON parseable, schema match. Minor: items uppercase (A/B/C vs a/b/c) — not a schema violation, noted.

probe_c_language.prompt: Briefly describe the ACID properties of a database transaction. Respond in English only.
probe_c_language.raw_response: A database transaction has four key properties that ensure data integrity and consistency: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability (ACID).
probe_c_language.notes: English, on-topic. Terse (single sentence) but technically correct.

probe_d_classify.prompt: Classify the following text into one of these categories: [bug, feature, documentation]. Text: 'Database connection drops after 30 seconds of idle.' Respond with just the category name.
probe_d_classify.notes: Correct classification. 0.2.13 W2.5 returned 'feature' 80% of the time — that bias is resolved on fixed dispatcher.

w2_concerns: Nemotron-specific stop tokens need research during W2
Identity confusion may affect roles that reference model identity in prompts
Very fast inference (0.09s classify) — suitable for triage/classification roles if identity issue is non-blocking
